title: Shared Sources, Tenant Subscriptions, State Machines, and Races · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers shared sources, tenant subscriptions, state machines, and races into a changed idempotent shared-source subscription service. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of idempotent shared-source subscription service with automated and judgment evidence. Learne…
objectives: Register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy.
Create and remove tenant-scoped follows without deleting shared source history and expose explicit desired-state semantics.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appealed transitions with actors and causes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Source Registration
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 8883 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile shared sources, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle transitions, authorization, and registration races; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Source Registration.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Source Registration, keep Feed Ts Source Registration connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
